panels.description: Wide shot: The narrator standing on a grassy hill, looking down at a ventilation shaft. Silhouettes of bronze gates in the distance. The mood is one of sudden intellectual clarity.

bubbles.text: My mind was in revolution. I finally understood the purpose of the wells, the towers, and the fate of my Time Machine.

panels.description: Medium close-up: The narrator pointing towards the dark entrance of a cavern. The sketch style emphasizes analytical observation. Split-panel overlay showing an owl and a pale cave fish as a visual comparison.

bubbles.text: The second species is subterranean. The bleached skin, the huge light-reflecting eyes—they are creatures of the dark.

panels.description: Cutaway diagram style: Cross-section of the earth beneath the narrator's feet showing a massive, complex network of tunnels and shafts beneath the surface landscape.

bubbles.text: The earth is tunnelled enormously. This is the factory of the Underworld.

panels.description: Medium shot: The narrator sitting on a rock, gesturing with hands as if explaining a complex economic theory. The background fades into a ghostly overlay of 19th-century factory chimneys transitioning into subways.

bubbles.text: It began as a widening gap between Capitalist and Labourer. Industry simply moved deeper to escape the surface.

panels.description: Close-up: The narrator's face, partially shadowed, looking intense and troubled. Reflections of a future divided into two distinct human species in his eyes.

bubbles.text: The Haves on the surface... and the Have-nots in the dark. A stratification that can never be undone.
